originalTitle: S&P 500, Nasdaq end up as chipmakers rebound
summary: STORY: U.S. stocks ended mostly higher Monday boosted by shares of chip companies. While the Dow fell slightly, the S&P 500 added three tenths of one percent and the Nasdaq climbed about nine tenths. :: Intel Intel shares jumped 11% after news website The Information reported that Alphabet's Google had placed an order to manufacture more than 3 million tensor processing units in 2028. :: Marvell Technology And shares of Marvell Tech rose 9.5% after S&P announced that the company would be added to the S&P 500 index later this month. Also, Iran and Israel said they had halted attacks on each other. This came after an appeal from U.S. President Donald Trump that they immediately "stop shooting." The attacks over 24 hours were the most direct confrontation between Iran and Israel since an April ceasefire in the war. Justin Livengood, senior portfolio manager with Invesco, says he thinks regardless of when there is an end to the conflict in the Middle East, energy prices could stay higher, which could lead the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates. “Inflation's been drifting up in general the last six to nine months, not just for oil prices but even for core elements. And that's a concern. I think that concern persists as oil and natural gas prices linger higher. And the longer that persists, the more likely we are to have a Fed feel they need to raise rates late this year. I certainly feel that right now the base case for the Fed is a hike, not a cut, as their next move. And I think with each week and month that this Middle East conflict continues. The odds of that hike get pulled forward.” :: Apple Other stocks on the move included Apple which dropped almost 2% after announcing a Siri revamp at its annual Worldwide Developers Conference. :: AWS And shares of Corning gained 5.5% after Amazon announced a multi-year multi-billion-dollar deal to buy the company's optical fiber and cables for its data centers.

Also, Iran and Israel said they had halted attacks on each other. This came after an appeal from U.S. President Donald Trump that they immediately "stop shooting." The attacks over 24 hours were the most direct confrontation between Iran and Israel since an April ceasefire in the war. Justin Livengood, senior portfolio manager with Invesco, says he thinks regardless of when there is an end to the conflict in the Middle East, energy prices could stay higher, which could lead the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates. “Inflation's been drifting up in general the last six to nine months, not just for oil prices but even for core elements. And that's a concern. I think that concern persists as oil and natural gas prices linger higher. And the longer that persists, the more likely we are to have a Fed feel they need to raise rates late this year. I certainly feel that right now the base case for the Fed is a hike, not a cut, as their next move. And I think with each week and month that this Middle East conflict continues. chineseTitle: 芯片制造商反弹，标普 500 指数和纳斯达克指数最终收高
chineseSummary: 故事：受芯片公司股票的提振，美国股市周一收盘大多走高。虽然道琼斯指数小幅下跌，但标准普尔 500 指数上涨了十分之三百分之一，纳斯达克指数上涨了约十分之九。 :: 英特尔 新闻网站 The Information 报道称，Alphabet 旗下的谷歌已下了 2028 年制造超过 300 万个张量处理单元的订单，英特尔股价大涨 11%。 :: Marvell Technology 在标普本月晚些时候宣布该公司将被纳入标普 500 指数后，Marvell Tech 股价上涨 9.5%。此外，伊朗和以色列表示，他们已停止对对方的攻击。此前，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普呼吁他们立即“停止射击”。此次袭击持续了 24 小时，是伊朗和以色列自 4 月份停火以来最直接的对抗。景顺(Invesco)高级投资组合经理贾斯汀·利文古德(Justin Livengood)表示，他认为无论中东冲突何时结束，能源价格都可能保持在较高水平，这可能导致美联储加息。 “过去六到九个月，通货膨胀总体上一直在上升，不仅是油价，甚至是核心要素。这是一个令人担忧的问题。我认为，随着石油和天然气价格持续走高，这种担忧持续存在。这种情况持续的时间越长，美联储就越有可能认为他们需要在今年晚些时候加息。我当然认为，目前美联储的下一步行动的基本情况是加息，而不是降息。我认为，中东冲突每周和每月都在发生。加息的可能性仍在继续。” :: 苹果 其他上涨的股票包括苹果公司，该公司在其年度全球开发者大会上宣布对 Siri 进行改进后，股价下跌近 2%。 :: AWS 和康宁股票上涨

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
